ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku higʊta bɩ tʊr deem ʊ Gadara zannɔ kʊ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, a m bɩ, gʊɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ kʊ zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ n bɔ gəənoyaarɔ ʊ, ŋnɩ b'a zɛ ʊ. Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ duro nyɩnta bʊnyaa gʊta. Naa minto bɩ, gɔsɩ b'a dam ma, an cem lɛɛ bɩ do ʊ y.
translated: Jesus, the one who was baptized by John the Baptist in the River Jordan, came from Gadara. He began preaching the good news, teaching people to repent and follow the way of God. Many followed him, and he taught them about righteousness and the kingdom of heaven.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: King Herod, when he was reigning in Judea at that time, during the rule of the Roman emperor. Then, there came wise men from the east to Jerusalem, saying, "Where is the one born king of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east and have come to worship him." When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. He assembled the chief priests and scribes, asking them where the Messiah was to be born.

ref: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
text: b'a hɩ a heer ʊ, a ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ yɩ m wusigəre m bɩncɛ gusire ʊ.› K'a yaa leeku bɩ, a yaa dɩga a gɔsɩ n a so cɛ b'ʊ, bɩ dintim ba b'ʊ sɔ y. Hɔ haay ɩ cɛ b'ʊ, a bɩncɛrɔ ʊ mɩŋŋa.
translated: Mother, you have come here, you: "Mama, I have gone to the market to buy salt for us." The child has just returned, and you see that she is carrying something on her head. Look at her, the child is walking carefully.

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: A n bɔkarɛ, an a bɔ, an nɩ dɩgam gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nyɩnta ŋnɩ bɩr ma rɔ wɔɔ ma, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ da bɩ ba n nɩ, kan mɔɔ danyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ!
translated: He went to the market, he was there, but when he tried to buy beans, he didn’t have enough money, so he returned home and said to his mother: "Mother, give me some money, because our food is finished!"

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: A nyarɛ ma bɩ, a nyɩyar a nyɔɔ, an n'a hɔɔn darɛ, a ʊ, kʊ mɔɔ nyɩ m naa, ŋ y'a daŋ ʊ.
translated: He went to the water there, he drank and rested, his home is far away, he, you, to come here with me, and you will see it.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: Thus, the great gathering is here: the people are in the outer area, the people are in the inner area, and the people are in the courtyard. The elders Zɩ and we gather here to decide! Elders, is this the way we must assemble the hɔka?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»
translated: Then he said, "You who have been healed by the Lord's mercy, listen to me, Jesus: Go to the place where you were healed, and there you will find."

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: Zibəənyɩ prepared the nyɩntam and mimbirenyɩntazaa, and he told Wusigə that he would come here, so he went to the place where Zibəə was to be with them.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»
translated: Bɩ said, "Jim, where are you going? Because Elii is coming to Bumbu!"

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: K'ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ lɛ kʊsɩrɛ, k'ɩ hi da ɩ meer ʊ, ɩ tidəər zɛ ɩ mim ma,
translated: K'ibii comes here to Kusire, and there he meets you, he sees the water and drinks it.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Lʊ bɩ k'a Yeezuu mim ma bɩ, a bʊr a kur zamaa gaa ʊ b'ʊ, an a wɔ da a huu ma.
translated: The child of Jesus, my mother's child, comes to bring peace and gives it, and the one who knows the time sees it.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Ŋ hɔɔn ba ʊ rɔ wɔɔ n'a hɩm, ŋ ʊ: ‹K'a nyɔɔ bɩ ka wɔɔ ʊ poore, bala, wɔɔ fɩtɩrɛrɔ wɔɔ ɩ lihim.›
translated: He said, "Your people have gone to the forest, but the village remains here."

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa a so durnya hɔ haay ʊ, an bɔkarɛ an kʊrɔ mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ ma, a nyɔɔdʊ ɩ ka? Gɛɛ gʊaa y'a mɩsɩrbaa lʊrm kan bɔ kɩ?
translated: Bi, I have heard that the world is like this, oh! I brought my own miskiba to the nyarɛ people, but why do you ask me? Do you know the truth? Where is the path to miskiba going?

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n Yeezuu gaa, an bɔ m toore ma, an nɩ nyaa bam kan kɩ, a ʊ. «Woso dɔrɛ ɩ ma kan naa kɩ Zuuba, mim naa do b'ɩ b'ɩ yɩrɛ y.»
translated: Peter said to Jesus, "You have the words of eternal life. We have believed and know that you are the Holy One of God."
